How to Write a Perfect Sympathy Card

30 real sympathy messages you can copy-paste today. 5 pro writing tips from our card editors. Bad-vs-good comparisons so you can spot the generic stuff and write something they'll actually remember.

📝 30 copy-paste messages 💡 5 writing tips ✅ Bad vs Good examples ❓ FAQs answered
Jump to: 30 Messages 5 Writing Tips Bad vs Good FAQ Pick a Sympathy Template

1 30 Sympathy Messages You Can Steal Today

Click 📋 Copy to save to clipboard. Click 🎴 Use on a card to open a sympathy template with this message ready to go.

01 ✍️ General
🕯️ With deepest sympathy. Holding you close in my heart during this sad time.
🎴 Use on a card
02 ✍️ General
I'm so sorry for your loss. Sending you all my love and strength. 🙏💙
🎴 Use on a card
03 ✍️ General
May your heart find peace and healing. With sincerest sympathy. 🌸
🎴 Use on a card
04 ✍️ General
So sorry for your loss. They were one of the good ones. 🕯️💛
🎴 Use on a card
05 ✍️ General
I'm here for you — anything you need, day or night. With love and sympathy. 🤗💙
🎴 Use on a card
06 ✍️ General
May your happy memories comfort you in the days ahead. Sending love. 💗
🎴 Use on a card
07 👩 For Her
I'm so sorry, my love. I can't imagine the pain you're feeling. I'm right here. 🫂💙
🎴 Use on a card
08 👩 For Her
To my dear friend, with deepest sympathy. Grief is love with nowhere to go. 💔💗
🎴 Use on a card
09 👩 For Her
I'm so sorry for your loss. Sending you the gentlest hug today. 🌸🤗
🎴 Use on a card
10 👩 For Her
With sympathy and love. Take all the time you need to grieve. 💛
🎴 Use on a card
11 👩 For Her
I know there are no words right now. Just know I love you. 💙🕯️
🎴 Use on a card
12 👩 For Her
So deeply sorry for your loss. Your mom/dad/sister was one in a million. 💗
🎴 Use on a card
13 👨 For Him
Dude, I'm so sorry for your loss. If you need anything at all, I'm here. 🙏🤗
🎴 Use on a card
14 👨 For Him
Brother, I'm so sorry. I love you and I'm thinking of you. 💙🕯️
🎴 Use on a card
15 👨 For Him
My deepest sympathy to you and your family. Just call, I'll come running. 🫂
🎴 Use on a card
16 👨 For Him
To my husband/boyfriend — I'm so sorry for your loss. Grieve in your own way, I'm beside you always. 💙💗
🎴 Use on a card
17 👨 For Him
With sympathy. Men cry too, and there's no shame in it. I love you. 💧❤️
🎴 Use on a card
18 👨 For Him
So sorry, brother. Let's get coffee and just talk. Or not. I'm here either way. ☕🤗💙
🎴 Use on a card
19 👨👩👧 Family
With deepest sympathy to our whole family. We'll get through this together. 🕯️💙
🎴 Use on a card
20 👨👩👧 Family
I'm so sorry for our loss. Their love will live on through all of us. 🌸💗
🎴 Use on a card
21 👨👩👧 Family
To my family: sending strength to everyone right now. I love you all. 🤗💙
🎴 Use on a card
22 👨👩👧 Family
With sympathy — our wonderful [Mom/Dad/Grandpa] will never be forgotten. 💛🕯️
🎴 Use on a card
23 👨👩👧 Family
Sending all my love to the family during this hard time. 🙏💗
🎴 Use on a card
24 👨👩👧 Family
Our hearts are joined in sorrow and in beautiful memories. With sympathy. 💙🕊️
🎴 Use on a card
25 👯 Friends
I'm so sorry for your loss, friend. Let me bring food, clean, anything. I got you. 🍲🤗💙
🎴 Use on a card
26 👯 Friends
Dude, my heart aches for you. So sorry for your loss. I love you, man. 💙💛
🎴 Use on a card
27 👯 Friends
To my bestie — I'm right here through all of it. With sympathy and a big hug. 🫂💗
🎴 Use on a card
28 👯 Friends
I'm so sorry. Grief isn't linear, I'll check on you in 6 months too. Love you. 🌸🙏💙
🎴 Use on a card
29 👯 Friends
So deeply sorry. Whatever you need — I'm there. 🕯️💞
🎴 Use on a card
30 👯 Friends
With love and sympathy. Your dad/mom was legendary and I'll never forget them. 💛
🎴 Use on a card

2 5 Pro Tips for Writing Sympathy Cards That Get Saved

After editing 10,000+ greeting cards, these are the 5 patterns that separate "nice" cards from "I kept this on my fridge for 3 years" cards.

  1. 💡 Start with 'I'm so sorry for your loss.' — open, direct, no poetry.
  2. 💡 Acknowledge that words are useless right now ('There truly are no words…').
  3. 💡 Offer SPECIFIC help (I'll bring lasagna Friday, I'll take the dog Mondays).
  4. 💡 Name the person who died ('Your dad was one of the greats').
  5. 💡 Add 'No need to reply.' — grieving people owe nothing.

3 Bad vs Good: Sympathy Card Messages, Side by Side

The difference between a forgotten card and a remembered one is usually 2 specific sentences. Here's what that looks like in practice.

❌ Don't write this
Everything happens for a reason.
✅ Write this instead
There truly are no words for how unfair this feels. I'm so, so sorry for your loss.
❌ Don't write this
They're in a better place.
✅ Write this instead
Your dad was one of the funniest, kindest men I ever met. I'll never forget his terrible BBQ jokes. He's so loved. 💙🕯️
❌ Don't write this
Let me know if you need anything.
✅ Write this instead
I'm dropping dinner off Wednesday at 6. No need to answer the door. No need to reply. ❤️

4 Sympathy Card Writing — FAQ

The questions we get asked 4–5 times a week about writing sympathy cards.

Is it okay to say "I know how you feel"?

No. You don't. Even if you lost someone too — every grief is different. Say "I can't imagine how this hurts" instead.

Should I mention the person who died by name?

YES. Hearing their name is healing. "Your mum was incredible — I'll never forget her scones" > vague "thinking of you".

Short note or long letter?

Short for acquaintances, long for close family/friends. Short = 2–3 lines is plenty. Don't ramble out of obligation.

Is a card enough or should I do more?

Card + 1 small, specific action is the combo. "I'm dropping dinner Thursday" > open offer of "let me know if you need anything".

5 Pick a Sympathy Template & Send in 60 Seconds

All templates are free to design, personalise, and preview. No account needed.
